Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The European AllSky7 fireball network 109 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Can It Resolve DOOM? Game Engine in 2k DNS Records 21 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Embracing Bayesian methods in clinical trials 39 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Explore the Hidden World of Sand 127 upvotes, 29 comments
  5. Browser-based SFX synthesizer using WASM/Zig 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Meow.camera 98 upvotes, 22 comments
  7. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate on a Brother printer with Certbot 156 upvotes, 43 comments
  8. Building FireStriker: Making Civic Tech Free 50 upvotes, 10 comments
  9. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 28 upvotes, 4 comments
  10. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 255 upvotes, 101 comments
  11. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 88 upvotes, 32 comments
  12. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 274 upvotes, 139 comments
  13. Some uncomfortable truths about AI coding agents 29 upvotes, 9 comments
  14. A Faster Alternative to Jq 343 upvotes, 216 comments
  15. Make macOS consistently bad (unironically) 91 upvotes, 50 comments
  16. Ask HN: Founders of estonian e-businesses – is it worth it? 38 upvotes, 19 comments
  17. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 49 upvotes, 27 comments
  18. AI got the blame for the Iran school bombing. The truth is more worrying 232 upvotes, 173 comments
  19. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 148 upvotes, 107 comments
  20. Hold on to Your Hardware 509 upvotes, 421 comments
  21. The 'paperwork flood': How I drowned a bureaucrat before dinner 476 upvotes, 396 comments
  22. Schedule tasks on the web 266 upvotes, 217 comments
  23. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 359 upvotes, 307 comments
  24. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 135 upvotes, 128 comments
  25. Show HN: Open-Source Animal Crossing–Style UI for Claude Code Agents 17 upvotes, 10 comments
  26. TurboQuant: Building a Sub-Byte KV Cache Quantizer from Paper to Production 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Solving Semantle with the Wrong Embeddings 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu domains 47 upvotes, 68 comments
  29. Should QA exist? 65 upvotes, 103 comments
  30. Telnyx package compromised on PyPI 22 upvotes, 52 comments

Last generated 4:30am, 28 March 2026. Source here.
